items.title: New SNAP work requirement rules expand time limit to older adults and some caregivers
items.summary: NPR reports that the time limit tied to SNAP work rules (often described as a three-month limit for many adults without qualifying work) has been extended from ages 18–54 to include adults up to age 65, and now also applies to caregivers of children as young as 14. NPR also says exemptions previously applied to veterans, people experiencing homelessness, and former foster youth were removed under the new rules, and households can lose benefits when adults are cut off.

items.title: KFF: States preparing for Medicaid work requirements starting Jan. 1, 2027; Nebraska to start May 1, 2026
items.summary: KFF says the 2025 reconciliation law’s Medicaid changes are estimated to increase the number of uninsured people by 7.5 million by 2034, with 5.3 million of that attributed to new work requirements for ACA Medicaid expansion (and certain waiver) adults beginning Jan. 1, 2027. KFF also notes Nebraska announced it will begin enforcing federal work requirements early starting May 1, 2026.

items.title: CMS guidance outlines Medicaid eligibility redeterminations for certain beneficiaries
items.summary: The Alliance of Safety-Net Hospitals reports CMS issued formal guidance directing states on how to handle more frequent Medicaid eligibility redeterminations under recent federal law, including relying first on ex parte information (like SSA data) and sending prepopulated renewal forms when needed. The report says affected individuals must receive advance notice and fair hearing rights at least 10 days prior to termination.

items.title: USDA WIC memo: states must allow non-fresh fruit/veg options by April 2026; FY26 CVV inflation update
items.summary: USDA FNS says WIC state agencies must authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026. FNS also says the FY 2026 cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) inflation adjustment is 11.8% over the FY 2022 baseline, resulting in a $1 increase for pregnant and postpartum participants (with no change for children and breastfeeding participants).

items.title: IRS opens 2026 tax filing season; April 15 deadline and refundable credit refund timing
items.summary: The IRS says it opened the 2026 filing season on Jan. 26, 2026 and expects returns to be filed ahead of the Wednesday, April 15 federal deadline. The IRS also says most early filers who claim EITC or ACTC and choose direct deposit should receive refunds by March 2, 2026 (assuming no issues), with Where’s My Refund showing projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: IRS: EITC/ACTC refunds can’t be issued before mid-February; Where’s My Refund updates by Feb. 21
items.summary: The IRS says that by law it can’t issue refunds for returns claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) before mid-February, and the entire refund is held (not just the credit portion). The IRS says Where’s My Refund should show an updated status by Feb. 21 for most early EITC/ACTC filers and that refunds are expected by March 2 if the return was filed online, direct deposit was selected, and there are no issues.

items.title: FY2026 HUD funding summary: $600M Tenant Protection Vouchers; can be used to support Emergency Housing Voucher households
items.summary: Bipartisan Policy Center’s summary of the enacted FY2026 THUD bill says it includes $600 million for new Tenant Protection Vouchers (TPVs), an increase of $263 million from FY2025. The summary also notes the bill allows housing authorities to use TPVs for households currently assisted by the Emergency Housing Voucher program.

items.title: HUD notice updates Emergency Housing Voucher service fee recapture and portability policy
items.summary: NAHRO reports HUD published Notice PIH 2026-02 describing how unobligated EHV service fee funds may be recaptured and reallocated to cover 2026 Housing Assistance Payment (HAP) and administrative fee shortfalls. NAHRO also says the notice revises EHV portability policy so agencies billing ported EHVs can absorb those vouchers into their regular Housing Choice Voucher program.

items.title: SSA: 2026 COLA is 2.8% for Social Security and SSI
items.summary: The Social Security Administration says Social Security and SSI benefits will increase 2.8% in 2026. SSA says the COLA begins with Social Security benefits payable in January 2026, and increased SSI payments begin on Dec. 31, 2025.

items.title: Taxpayer Advocate Service: Key 2026 tax dates include April 15 filing deadline
items.summary: The IRS Taxpayer Advocate Service lists April 15 as the due date for individual taxes (or for requesting an extension) in its 2026 tax dates guide. The guide also lists other key dates like March 16 for partnerships and S-corporations and June 15 for certain taxpayers abroad.

items.title: Federal Student Aid: 2025–26 FAFSA must be received by June 30, 2026
items.summary: The Federal Student Aid 2025–26 FAFSA form says the application must be received no later than June 30, 2026 for the 2025–26 award year. The award year runs from July 1, 2025 through June 30, 2026.
